mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks such as text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first AI solution with decentralized inference capabilities.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing LocalAI resources.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the downloaded binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step sets up a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI to the cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI using a Helm chart, which simplifies the installation process.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI in the 'localai' namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall LocalAI from the specified namespace.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade LocalAI to a newer version, including backup and verification.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o json > localai-backup.json

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Backup current LocalAI pod configurations.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o json > localai-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' and adjust configurations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Kubernetes cluster is running with 'minikube status'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for LocalAI.

**Fix:**
Increase the CPU and memory limits in the Minikube configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved API endpoints for text generation.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy model support; users must migrate to the new model format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.10.x, ensure to update your model configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
